methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $72M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Lumotive is a Series B semiconductor chip company that has raised $72M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a universe of 8 private peers in chips and semiconductors at similar or adjacent stages—including Positron AI, Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, and DEEPX—alongside 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $137M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage privates produced $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 24.4x median price-to-sales multiple was not executable due to absence of Lumotive's revenue data. These two methods triangulate to a low of $116M, median of $568M, and high of $1.1B.

The wide gap between stage-progression ($137M) and peer-median ($1.0B) reflects material uncertainty around market timing and capital efficiency relative to this peer cohort. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively to capital deployed; the peer-median incorporates recent market pricing for frontier-chip companies but includes wide dispersion. A credible range is $400M–$800M, with key uncertainties centered on revenue trajectory, proprietary technology differentiation, and the sustainability of current-market valuations in the AI-chip sector.
